title: Determining Protein Concentration of Cell-free Extract
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"217a7","text":"Cell-free protein synthesis reactions require high-quality, active extracts to produce high yields of protein therapeutics, enzymes, antigens, vaccines, and other proteins. One way of assessing extract quality and lysis efficiency during cell-free extract preparation is to determine its overall protein concentration. This can be done using a Bradford assay.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5tn24","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dmsg1","text":"Read more on cell-free protein synthesis here: https://www.swiftscalebio.com/blog/cell-free-protein-synthesis ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":47,"length":62}],"data":{}},{"key":"ljf4","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9v0h3","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b2hkh","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ebc3a","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ef2fp","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ccfa5","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"CB87DC20C88B11EBBBFC970C7085C639","url":"https://www.swiftscalebio.com/blog/cell-free-protein-synthesis"}}}}

steps: In 96 well plate with lid 3370 add 250 µL of Bradford Reagent to each well.
Dilute BSA standards according to the following:
Make a serial dilution of extract (expected concentration of cell-free extract is ~ 40 mg/mL ).
Measure all samples in triplicate [(# of samples+ 8 standards)*3=# of wells used].
Add 5 µL of sample/standard to each well containing Bradford Reagent.
Place plate on plate reader (must be able to read absorbance at 595 nm).
a. Turn on lamp and allow to warm up.
b. Set which wells to read in both protocol and procedure.

c. Plot standard curve using preferred software and use to calculate protein concentration from sample signal and dilution factor. Expected Result : Expected concentration of protein in Cell-free extract is ~ 40 mg/mL so make the following dilutions:
creator: Emily Pennell
